reviews.review_text:
Spent the first 2 years trying to do everything and never having enough time. 

Days upon days of constantly running around trying to find the town folk who asked for help and celebrating every festival/birthday only to pass out at 2am and have my pockets molested.

Things are way different in the DPRK.
Thanks to the Glorious Leaders Immaculate Initiative to stop crime we don't deal with things like this.

I knew I beat the game when I gifted enough alcohol and cake to give the whole town liver failure and diabetes so the Racoon family could invade.

Stardew Valley was one of those games that I had heard about for years and simply had no interest in. It isn't my style, it isn't the type of content I play at all. But one day I was so unbelievably bored that I thought, f*ck it- let's play Stardew Valley. My friends all love it so I might as well.

 I logged 14 hours the first time I opened the game.

The thing is, I could play this game on repeat forever and doesn't even grace my top 3 games. 

It's fun, addictive, wholesome, somewhat dark and even frustrating. The townspeople feel so genuine to be around. I love getting to know them all over again in every run on the game. I'm aiming for 1 save per marriage candidate. 
I love designing my farm, the feeling of euphoria when you reach Ginger Island for the first time... your first batch of starfruit jelly....
I won't consider this a con on the game, but for some reason this is the ONLY game on my pc that crashes. Nothing else, save for maybe a sims crash every fortnight. Anyway. 

Really fun. Going to get a tattoo of a fairy rose when I can.

The only actual cons I can think of is a lack of new diaglouge and events once you finish year 2. Maybe need some new townies or something. Fix Shane's alcoholism after you marry him or something
my dog's passing really allowed me to start enjoying the relaxing parts of life. cheers to more Stardew Valley.

Stardew is the king of cozy games. Sublime pixel art, satisfying gameplay, loveable characters & enchanting music. The fact that it was all created by one guy is just mind-blowing. This is an experience that’ll stick with you for the rest of your life; and nestle itself deep within a little corner of your heart.

An amazing game that's constantly updated, done almost entirely by a single developer until coding for multiplayer became a bit too tedious. When I say everything, I mean absolutely everything. The music, the graphics, the coding, which is a feat in and of itself. There's plenty of videos detailing the biography behind the development of this game, one more inspiring than the next.

The game itself is simple yet unique and can get intrinsically complex with how to optimize or simplify things. There's plenty of ways to make money, solidify relationships, or simply enjoy music and events in the game.

In essence, it's a farming game that plays on old Harvest Moon vibes, with much more in almost every aspect. This has a niche for every style of gameplay you could ask for. My nieces play it in my car. My Uncles play it in their free time, we always compare our farms whenever we have family gatherings. There's a magic to it that encapsulates every audience, whatever you choose to do in game.
good for casual play but if u wanna go the completionist route.. u better lube ur ass beforehand coz its about to be blown wide open

I bought this game about 10 years ago.
In the beginning i did not play it that much, as I liked fast paced games more. 
Since then, life has happened, but I've had great games to relax with, like Minecraft, and Stardew Valley.
I have played it on console with friends when they visit me, with friends on pc when we are far apart, and played it to wind down from a long day of work.  

The developer cares about the game, and the community, and updates are free.
Cheap on the store, best 10 dollars I've ever spent.

I LOVE this game. It's cute, charming and heart warming. I always come back to it when I need a good game to play. Plus the developer of the game seems like a genuinely good guy. 10/10 highly recommend

All right, after so many years playing Stardew Valley in various platforms and only NOW managing to reach the Perfection Achievement, I can say confidently that this is a good game. This is a very good game. This is one of the best games that have been created. 10/10 all around. Certainly, no one is waiting for one more person to say this, but after spending so many hours, I feel obligated to leave this review, as this is more than a farming sim. It is truly a heartwarming adventure (with a dash of extreme greed that may or may not consume you; it probably will).

SV is the goat, kthhnx bai
Very addicting, would say i personally like the game more w frens :P
This game is cozy and surprisingly deep. Next thing you know, apparently it's been 109 hours.

Stardew Valley is my favorite game in the entire world. If I had to pick only one game to play for the rest of my life, it would be this one without hesitation.

Whether I'm farming, fishing, mining, decorating my farm, raising animals, making friends with the townspeople, or just enjoying the peaceful atmosphere, there's always something to do. Every playthrough feels a little different, and somehow I still find new things to discover even after countless hours.

It's cozy when I need to relax, challenging when I want goals to work toward, and endlessly replayable. The music is beautiful, the characters are memorable, and the amount of love and care that went into this game is obvious in every detail.

This isn't just one of my favorite games—it's my favorite game of all time. I can't recommend it enough.

❤️ 10/10, would happily spend the rest of my life on my farm.

As one of three people in the world not forced to play this game by a woman with BPD who would later cheat on and traumatize me, i can approach reviewing this game without any bias and will say that this game is a complete, wonderful, wholesome, masterpiece.

10/10

STARDEW VALLEY.
one of the most games of all time8)
great solo
so much better with friends

there was this time on my 2nd attempt at a send, where I went to the flower dance to find love; only for the whole town to turn me and my flowers down.....
ALSO why can't I Marry/romance my friends then :c or can I? xD
anyways I finally made it out the first year (yeahh I know lacking right)
will add to this review as I slowly get into the "end game" and start smashing these achievements 
but since revisiting and getting deeper its a heavy reminder how great this game is 
I plan to complete it one day with the fav

One of the best indie games!

There is a lot of content and secrets in the game to explore.

Best game for casual gamers or people new to games. Very relaxing and so fun to play, SV is a good showcase how simple but not banal game can hold you for dozens of hours
I'm about to end second in-game year and I'm sure that there will be many more in-game years on my farm
10/10

This is a cutsey slow game where you have to collect items and build connections with NPCs, each have their own personalities. You spend your time farming, foraging, mining or fishing. It is a relaxing game, 10/10 if you are just getting into gaming or want a game you can build a life in that you will ultiately care for.
I love love love this game! this is how me and my friends keep in touch even when we cant see each other its so fun and I love the cute animals.

I've played this game a few different times on Nintendo Switch Lite, and now for the first time on PC. I love the feeling of being in a community while forging my own adventure. Stardew Valley is a way to explore different ways of living with varying skills of focus and goals. I hope that playing this game on PC allows me to play with other players online. I have a feeling that, with other players, this game will continue to impress and be engaging.

I mean I like it, nice slow paced game, but for me it gets a bit boring after a while. I think I’ll give it another go though. Again, personal preference, because the game itself is actually really good for people that somehow have unlimited free time i guess. Can definitely recommend it as daft as that sounds

This game is legit peak.
no matter how many times I play it I always find myself coming back to it
its the perfect comfort game you can farm fish mine and even have relationships with the NPCs.
Every time I'm exhausted or bored from competitive games I find myself coming back here to chill relax and have fun
(only stressful thing is running out of energy in the mines and not having enough time to get home)
I played this with my beautiful boyfrienddd I love him so much, hes the greatest and hes allll mine. Also the game was good ig

Before I played Stardew Valley, I never thought pixel art could captivate me. I always gravitated toward modern, realistic graphics, the kind that try to impress you with detail and fidelity. But Stardew Valley changed that completely. Its simplicity isn’t a limitation, it’s a kind of magic. The pixel art feels warm, nostalgic, and alive in a way I didn’t expect, and it opened my eyes to an entire style of games I used to overlook.

I love this game so much that I actually own three copies, one on Switch, one on PC, and one on mobile. Even though I haven’t played much on PC or mobile yet, my Switch farm has over 500 hours poured into it. It’s perfected, polished, and complete… and yet, I still feel the pull to go back. There’s something comforting about stepping onto that farm again, hearing the soft music, and slipping back into the rhythm of planting, harvesting, mining, and connecting with the townsfolk.

What makes Stardew Valley special is how personal it becomes. It doesn’t rush you. It doesn’t demand anything. It simply invites you to build a life at your own pace and somehow, that quiet freedom becomes deeply meaningful. Whether it’s the changing seasons, the relationships you build, or the satisfaction of watching your farm evolve, the game creates a sense of home that’s rare to find.

Even after “finishing” it, I still want to revisit it often. That’s the mark of a truly great game: one that stays with you long after the goals are completed.
Lot of love went into this game. It is simple, but rewarding to play
I love this game so much! Its super chill and you can play at your own pace.
Super fun and cozy! Takes tons of time to finish and lots to do!

I really enjoy this game.  I have found myself playing this game going on several years now.  I love it and I can play with my Hubby and Daughter as well which is an added bonus since my daughter and I live in different states.  I enjoy changing the layout of my farm and meeting my goals and I also enjoy watching other peoples Stardew farms on YouTube and getting ideas.
you don't know how fast i bought the game when i saw it on sale i have the game on android but this feels much better
200+ hrs in and still playing. Good game, better game with friends.
This is the greatest game ever made, ever. No doubt. Zero hesitation. I love you Concerned Ape.

I absolutely love Stardew Valley! 🌱✨
It’s such a cozy and relaxing game with so much to do. From farming and fishing to exploring the mines and making friends in town, every day feels fun and rewarding. The pixel art and soundtrack are beautiful, and the game gives you complete freedom to play at your own pace. It’s the perfect escape from stress and one of the best indie games ever made.
such a peak game to play while you pass time. Honestly theres so much to do it actually never ends but in a good way

At first I did not enjoy this game, I thought of it as boring and slow paced (which it kinda is) but after around 20 hours (and speeding up the game with cheat engine) I actually enjoyed it, it grows on you after a while its like a 9/10 game. I'm gonna marry Haley or Penny.

The feeling this game gave me when I played it for the first time is indescribable! I haven't had felt that amount of joy for so long... a missed feeling that was brought back to me by a game I had no idea I would be playing someday. 
10/10 , I bought it for two friends and recommended it to many others. can't wait for future updates!

If you are reading this comment before buying the game: Do it ! you'll never regret the decision <3
The ultimate cure for depression and the perfect co-op game for couples. There is no in-between. Easily one of the best and most comforting life-sims out there.

easily my favorite game of all time with many more hours on other platforms. play with friends and mods for the ultimate gameplay (tractor mod ily). the developer is incredible and continues to give free updates to the game making every restart fresh and exciting:)

The gameplay took a minute to get to grips with, but is soooo engaging and enjoyable. I'd gone into it expecting a sort of terraria meets minecraft type of deal, but the lore is such an awesome surprise! Would 1000% recommend this game to others, I can't wait to play more.

I had this game originally on the switch and now I bought it for steam. This game is one that I always come back to, no matter how long it has been since I last played it. It perfectly encapsulates the vibes of the seasons through the environment, music, and festivals for each one.  There is so much to this game that allows for many replays. You can try out different farm types and designs, experience relationships with various characters and, if you're strong enough, abandon the small town vibes for the corporate Joja. If you're into perfecting games, this one has so much to collect and complete to eventually reach that perfection. One of the things that gives this game so much longevity is the ability to play it in small or long chunks. Sometimes I want to sit and play for a few hours, and sometimes I just want to play one in-game day when I have the time. It makes it easy to keep coming back to the game each day. I absolutely adore this game and would recommend it to anyone.

After only 2.7 hours in Stardew Valley, I’ve already spent most of my time fishing like my life depends on it, planted a few crops, met some weirdly lovable villagers, and somehow lost track of time completely.
This game is dangerously relaxing and honestly way too addictive already. Absolutely loving it so far.

This is my all time favorite game due to the variety it gives of things to do while playing.  All the goals are easily obtainable but not too easy, the scenery and ambient noise are relaxing and you could play forever due to it's how it is set up.  The things i feel it could add is an elevator in the Skull Caverns if only because I would love to see how far I could get, oodles of challenges on the post in front of the mayors office because I can never have too many, more variety of the ocean, river and lake fish, recipes are always good to add on to, crops and animals basically lots more to collect while playing.  Thank you so much for making this game it really has helped in a time of need.
10/10 will forever recommend Stardew Valley. This game holds a special place in my heart and is in my top 5 favorite games to play <3
Nice dating sim, the farming minigame is okay too I guess...

Game is incredibly good. Story is memorable, characters are likable, developer pays a lot of attention to this game even several years after launch.
It is a must play, definitely.
I've played the game for 200 hours and am nowhere near having seen all the content offered. This game is amazing.

I originally bought Stardew Valley a few years back on my switch and it quickly became one of my favorite games to spend hours of my time on. I share the switch with my brother so now that he's currently keeping it, I recently went on to buy it again on here just so that I could play more, and I already have 100 hours just on Steam. I even bought it for my friend because I think it's really a game worth spending money on, and I was happy both to share it with someone else and to support ConcernedApe the goat because he's not a big fat greedy major studio o7 ! (where is this music coming from?)
I've always kind of rushed my gameplays in Stardew, and it took me a while to understand that there wasn't any huge benefit to playing that way, when the game literally punishes you for overexerting yourself etc.. I don't know how many people need to hear this but I think that Stardew doesn't need to be played that way, and I would honestly recommend all other casual players to take it easy. In the end, there's no need to rush for the community center in year 1 and then perfection as soon as possible, and I realised that once I forced myself to slow down a little when playing. Of course, having one or two min max saves is fun, but I'd say to give yourself an opportunity to play it like the """"cozy"""" game ppl claim it is (I hate that word but.)
Haunted Chocolatier tomorrow ok bye ,

Amazing game, can't stop playing it. It's basically completely self playable so far without having to look up any guides. Just enjoy exploring the games mechanics without spoilers and you'll have a blast.

Amazing game, had played it before on xbox and on my phone but its really more comfortable to play on keyboard, it was designed with that it mind and it is pretty clear. Chill game to just relax for a while, and the soundrack really helps you relax and just forget about everything else

i'm only playing this game because my friends & boyfriend force me to... Q_Q

jokes aside, it's a fun game to play together with people who enjoy it, but as someone who is more of an animal crossing type of person, the lenght of the days, fear of passing out outside and the rush to complete everything needed in time doesn't make this game cozy at all, but stressful q_q same thing with the stores and buildings only being open during a small portion of the day. playing this game requires a suprising amount of grinding, which is not what i personally seek from something that seems like a cozy farm game

The NPCS need more dynamic dialogues that reflect your accomplishments. For example, if you start dating Haley, Emily or any other npc dont even acknowledge it. Also the reactions to gifts are the same no matter your relationship and amount of hearts. Furthermore, every npc only has like 8 different lines when you talk to then (Not considering the ones after you enter a relationship) and it gets old after a few seasons. It's still a great game but there's no reason not to add more dialogues.

I really enjoyed the game by far. I play it with my partner, and we really have a lot of fun time. If you are looking for a game that has a lot of fun events, journey and memories, this game is definitely for you. Start your own farm, make new friendships and overall: Have fun.

Every time I start to play this game, it goes well. Until I go into joja mart and see that depressed twink(?) stocking shelves. When he comes on screen, I do to, and I don't stop, I can't stop. I have been to the hospital 34 times. Remove him from the game. I can't even talk to him to find out his name, because I pass out just from his sprite. Eric Barone pay for my hospital bills!

Depending on my mood can either be hella chill or an exercise in optimization. The short day can be a little stressful if im in a min-maxing mood, but great if u generally have limited time(a day in SDV is 14m). The base game is wonderful and i sunk well over 200h into the mobile version before i started playing on PC, but mods elevate this ish. If you ever find urself getting bored theres so many wonderful mods to make it feel new again. Peak
Great game when you want to sit back, listen to youtube, and just grind.

very good, there is so many different aspects to the game and considering how cheap it is (especially when on sale) - I don't see how someone can dislike the game. It is practically a 6/10 at worse, no matter who you are and I would personally say its an 8 or 9

While everyone calls it a cozy game, it's pretty overwhelming in regards to the amount of stuff there is to do. But once you get past that, it's probably one of my favorite games to come back to, especially with friends. The combat is pretty fun, though the energy gauge stops you from really getting into a groove with it, but you can't be too upset when you've got some of the most charming NPCs that games have to offer. Ironically enough, it's the farming that's the least interesting part of this farming simulator. Keep a Wiki at the ready for which characters like which gifts, and then give it a go! I've got about 25 hours (so far) on my PS5 copy in addition to the stats I have on Steam.
Very good game overall, character designs, background, structures, backstories, everything is so good.
this game is cute and fun. There's not a lot of direction so that part is kind of nice because you can do what you want but also harder because there's very little instruction

if i had to play one game for the rest of my life, it would be probably this or valorant. However seeing as how stardew valley doesn't get me tilted because I whiffed all my shots, 9 times out of 10 you'd probably see me picking stardew valley. Aside from that, it's nice and worth every single penny.
I adore this game so much. I just wish you could befriend Gunther :( and date the wizard tbh. him or Gus

Great game.  Runs well on old hardware.  A large amount of mod support massively extends gameplay.  Everything from QoL to full rebuilds/expansions.  You can play the game at your own pace and however you want.

I love this game so much I bought it three times. Once on mobile, once on console, and now on PC. PC is undoubtedly the best version. This game feels like exactly what whatever you want it to be. You could go mining, or fishing, or even just stay home and water crops all day. Heck, you could even just chill in the bar and play journey of the prairie king. The Co-op in this game is incredible. Two of my friends and I recently started and there's so much room for competition or personal goals. Five star game, no doubt.

a shilll lovely game can be a bit overwelming but dont worry nothing you can loose or pass by without doing again you can play however you want it has sooo much content and secrets and atchivments but the best part that you can do whatever you want play however you want and still have funn you cant do anything wrong in this game <3

Honestly, This game has my whole heart. I adore cozy games and I enjoy a bit of combat in my games as well as time management. This has all that and more. The soundtrack is wonderful, the characters are fleshed out although some more than others but I haven't gotten 100% yet so I don't know for sure. Its fun solo or with friends and I think that's the best part
Nothing but love for Stardew Valley and Eric Barone (aka ConcernedApe). You need this in your game library.
The best game ever, Buy it. It is worth it if you are looking for a game that you will actually enjoy. I love evrything about; the story line, the playing, the genre etc

This game quickly became an all time favorite of mine. I have spent many hours (on switch and pc) playing with friends or just on my own. It's addictive and charming, and I think I'll keep playing this game for many years to come.

Yes, yes, yes! While I find it to be way less deep than some competitors (Long time Rune Factory fanboy here), this gives a nice chilled entry-level farm-slash-lifesim experience.

I play it on Steamdeck, personally, and it plays amazingly. Can't wait to try out the co-op mode.
Personally its just not my kind of game. It never feels like I'm getting more than a little bit done every day and it gets boring really fast.

WARNING. MOD TO YOUR HEARTS CONTENT!

I absolutely fell in love with Stardew Valley way back when, but with the modding scene I fell in love deeper than ever before! It's so easy to mod this game, and I will be the first to admit that I could never go back to vanilla... This game is truly a masterpiece and you should play if if you love farming sims!
Super peaceful and relaxing experience. Just right for playing with my daughter.
Super cozy, super cute gameplay, everything about this game is very pretty and nice to play.
Pleasantly surprised at how fun this game is to play alone. Cute characters, story, art, music, and fun goals.
Amazing Game! Highly recommend to anyone that wants a chill, relaxing game!
Perfect game for relaxing and taking a break from life. Playing alone or with friends. Also the community and everything around the game is 10/10.
The game gives you a lot of freedom in how you play it, low stakes but fun gameplay and subtle but enjoyable story.

Yeah you could say I like this game. It's the one game I return to again and again. The game on its own is already more than worth the price in play time. Especially with all the content that has been added since its release. Now, add to that the infinite adjustments you can add through mods made by the community and you can start to see how my playtime racked up into the thousands of hours! The game is good for just playing a day or two, or diving into it and completely losing track of time, highly recommend!
played for three days straight when i first bought it and got sick. worth it.

I started playing with a friend and thought I wouldn't like the game, but as I explored I slowly came to love it. At it's base it's a farming game but you can mine (which has combat), fish, and work to unlock secrets. Each character has their own stories that are really fun and often quirky. I would recommend this game for anyone who is looking for a relaxing game that you can play at your own pace.
very fun, is very good for people who want a story on the side with slow progressression, i have too many hours in this game and can comfirm its addictive
Taught me the meaning of friendship and made it possible for me to spend time with my beautiful wife... 10/10 game no comments

Stardew Valley is such a. charming, beautiful RPG with adorable, cozy pixel art, heart-warming dialogue and character interactions, and absolutely incredible gameplay with impressive easter eggs. 10/10 all the way!!

I’ve been playing this game while I’m in the hospital waiting for a heart transplant, this is the only thing keeping my mind at ease. Playing on my steam deck is the best.
I didn’t realize what kind of game it was until I got into it, and honestly do not regret it, allot to do, allot to explore, and definitely a lot of grinding.

Stardew Valley was my first video game so I'd think it'd be only right for it to also have my first review too. 

I got this game thinking that it was going to be super relaxing and peaceful. Boy was my first spring harvest excruciatingly stressful... I didn't even talk to any of the townsfolk until the next month. But when winter came I was blasted with the most epic snowy soundtrack I've ever listened to in all the games I've played. 

10/10 I will probably never get all the achievements and will never stop playing this game.

This is a fantastic game! It has opportunities for those who are looking for "strictly cozy," and those who are a bit more daring. There are lots of little easter eggs and secrets to find. The NPC development and storylines are quite good, considering the simple graphics. It was a memorable experience, for sure!

I first played this game in mobile after this I play this in PC. It has some differenciable gameplay 
1. we can do many task here
2. the relationship bar is very good
3. The graphics is cozy
Already put hundreds of hours into this game on consoles. Got it on here to finally try a modded playthrough and it's been great
Very addicting and good replay-ability, something most AAA studios fail to achieve
I like to play this game after I get off work at the farm to experience a world where every crop I plant produces and bindweed doesn't exist

This game was really breathtaking, and the experience was more than different than many other games feeling like your in another world with quests, and the artwork. It is such a prismatic gameplay with every step making more and more progress.
While this game is with friends my friends won't let me eat the legend. It is such a pain.

This game is the bomb. Since its release i keep coming back to play this game.
I have started a new save several times already! 
Its also extremely satisfying for people who love to platinum/100% games. 

Cant recommend it enough!
the coziest game ever. Playing with someone enhances the experience a lot but playing alone is not bad, highly suggest playing with someone.
pretty chill game, alot to do andd quite fun to play
Relaxing and fun to grow your farm and complete achievements/quests.

Definitely an amazing game. It's so cozy and genuinely fun to play. There are lots of different story lines and quests that keeps the game interesting. NPC's are fun to interact with and have cool heart events that lets you see into their story. 10/10
If YOU are thinking or hesitating to buy and play this don't. This game is worth beyond the price

Quite relaxing and enjoyable . I like very much the design,background ,charcaters joyful .
very good for play casually .I recommand it !

Best dating sim I ever played in my life. Great multiplayer too and it's fun to compete with your friends to see who can woo the villager first. On top of that the farming mechanics are good, there is lots of stuff to do in this game including fishing, mining, farming, and pursuing your love interest. 10/10

i truly have an insane amount of love for this game. it is truly one of my top most favorite games. one day i was bored and thought "ehhh i guess i will try stardew." now it is a true obsession. and a game that became such a comfort and helped me through so much. there is so much to do and so much to unlock. if you are thinking of getting this game i fully recommend you do. sidhfuhshefriuhew i just love this game so much.
There isn't anything I don't enjoy about this game but my favorite aspect, graphics aside, is the diversity of marriage choices.
